source_units.text_raw:
SERVICES
The City shall collect fees for permit applications and related services as outlined in a separate city fee schedule and as enabled by the Planning Act.
The value of the processing fees shall be set from time to time by a resolution of Council and may be amended by resolution as per the Municipal Government Act.
Fees shall be submitted with a completed application form for a:
Building permit, sign permit, footing permit, demolition permit, and moving permit;
Preliminary or final subdivision;
Variance, rezoning, design review, pre-app Inquiry, and planning enquiry;
Moving and demolition permit deposits; and
The value of cash-in-lieu of parking spaces and parkland.
No person shall undertake a development except as provided herein and in conformity with this bylaw and the Province Wide Minimum Development Standard Regulations as per the Planning Act and where there is lack of clarity the Province shall be consulted.
No Development shall be undertaken without a building permit.
No building construction, addition or renovation, subject to the Building Code Bylaw, shall be undertaken without a building permit.
Notwithstanding Part 2.2, a building permit is not required when:
Normal maintenance of the exterior of an existing building or structure (i.e. shingling, siding, roofing, repainting) excepting registered heritage properties;
Temporary uses or temporary signs as specified in Section 4.35.
The following or similar miscellaneous minor structures:
Clothes poles
Flag poles
Garden trellises
Cold frames
Fences
Retaining walls under 1.2 m high
Children's play structures
Doghouses
Monuments
Interpretive displays
Any other exemptions contained in the PEI Building Code.
Subject to subsection (8) of the Planning Act,
This bylaw enables development through 4 potential pathways:
As-of-Right Pathway: This process is fully administered by the Development Officer. Some as-of-right pathways may include conditions that must be met as set out in the zones.
Site Plan Approval Pathway: This process includes more rigorous built- form and application submission requirements for larger projects. This process is administered by the development officer unless a variance is required.
Development Agreement Pathway: The Development Agreement (DA) pathway requires approval by council and is more time intensive than the other pathways with less certainty of outcome. DA pathways are only permitted where specifically identified in this bylaw.
These development pathways are described more fully, including key review stages and timelines, and submission requirements in a separate PLANNING & HERITAGE DEPARTMENT CHECKLIST document which is periodically updated as may be required by the PEI Planning Act.
Each zone in this bylaw sets out the potential development pathways available.
Development Agreement pathways are limited to only:
Lots that are greater than 1 hectare in size within the Institutional (I), Airport Periphery (AP), Port (P) and Growth Node (GN) zones, and
Any lot in the Eastern Gateway (EG), and The Urban Expansion (UE) zones.
Gas Service Station developments
Every person proposing to Erect a building or undertake a Development shall, undertake a 2-stage building permit process.
Stage 1 , the zoning compliance review, shall ensure that the zoning and subdivision requirements of this bylaw are met prior to advancing to stage 2.
Stage 2 , the building code review, is designed to ensure the Building Code requirements are met, following on the approval of the Stage 1 review.
If an application for a building permit is incomplete, the Development Officer shall notify the applicant in writing within seven (7) calendar days of the submission.
If an application for a building permit that is incomplete and remains so for a period of six (6) months, the application is considered abandoned and thus cancelled, null and void.
Where the Development Officer is unable to determine if an application meets the requirements of this bylaw, or other bylaws or statutes which may be in force, the Development Officer may refer the application to the Planning Board or Heritage Board to give direction on the disposition of
When a stage 1 building permit application does not meet the minimum regulations of this bylaw, the applicant may apply for a variance to:
Reduce the minimum setback / stepback requirement for a front, rear, side and/or flankage yard by up to 15%;
Reduce the minimum regulation pertaining to lot area, coverage, parking counts, and lot frontage by up to 10%;
Increase the minimum building height by up to 10%, as long as the change is not intended to provide additional floor area than what might otherwise have been permitted;
Increase the building height following Section 2.10;
Reduce the minimum streetwall width, maximum building dimensions, articulation requirements, or other built-form standards by up to 10%; and
Signage requirements.
Council may initiate an amendment to the Zoning and Development Bylaw to change the text of the bylaw or to rezone a property without the authorization of the owner(s) of land(s) involved in the rezoning, provided that the proposed amendment obtains the support of Council and complies with the general intent and purpose of the Official Plan. If the proposed amendment is contrary to the policies in the Official Plan, an amendment to the Official Plan must be filed in-conjunction with or prior to the bylaw amendment.
A person who seeks to rezone a parcel of land, or to otherwise have this bylaw amended, shall submit an application that includes such information as may be required by the Development Officer for the purpose of adequately assessing the desirability of the proposal, and if the application is for a rezoning it shall include:
Sufficient funds to cover the cost associated with processing the application;
A legal description and a plot plan, or a survey plan, accurately showing the location of the property or properties to be rezoned;
The names and addresses of the owner of the property and, if the applicant is not the owner, a statement as to the applicant's interest in the property or statement of consent from the owner;
AMENDMENTS
Site specific zoning amendments for changes to dimensional zoning measures (frontage, height, parking count, tree planting etc.) shall be addressed through variance protocols and are otherwise not permitted on a site specific basis.
The purpose of this section is to permit additional building height, beyond the maximum height otherwise permitted in the applicable zone, where a development proposal demonstrably provides public benefits that advance municipal objectives.
Bonus height shall not be granted as-of- right, but shall be subject to review and approval by the Design Review Board as outlined in Section 2.12 and following the eligible public benefits criteria in Section 2.11;
Notwithstanding the maximum building height permitted in the applicable zone, a development may be permitted a maximum bonus of two (2) additional storeys, in the following zones:
Downtown Core (DC),
Downtown Waterfront (DW), and
Growth Node zones (GN).
An application for a Bonus height shall be submitted with sufficient information as may be required by the Development Officer for the purpose of adequately assessing the proposal, including:
A legal description and a plot plan, or a survey plan, accurately showing the location of the property and building(s) or structure(s) on the property in question;
The name and address of the owner(s) of the property and, if the applicant is not the owner, a statement as to the applicant's interest in the property;
Design drawings, as required to proceed through the design review process;
The drawings must show the affordable units being proposed and their total Gross Floor Area; and
Such additional information as deemed necessary by the Development Officer, including studies or analyses to ensure that the proposed Bonus Height and/or its massing meet the desired performance standards with respect to mitigating visual, shadow, wind, and traffic impacts.
Any additional height granted through this section shall be proportional and commensurate with the value of the public benefit provided as set out in Section 2.11.2;
Approval of bonus height under this section shall not be construed as creating a precedent or vested right, and each application shall be evaluated on its own merits having regard to site-specific conditions and municipal policy objectives.
BONUS HEIGHT
Bonus height may be considered only in exchange for the provision of one or more of the following public benefits, to the satisfaction of the City:
Affordable Housing
Affordable housing shall meet income, rent, and tenure requirements established by the City's housing policies or programs in effect at the time of application.
Affordable units shall be secured for a minimum period of 10 years, or such longer period as Council may require.
Public Art
Public art shall be publicly accessible and visible from a public street or publicly accessible open space.
Public art shall be designed, commissioned, and installed in accordance with City-approved public art guidelines.
Cash-in-lieu contributions may be permitted where physical installation is not feasible, at Council's discretion.
Publicly Accessible Indoor or Outdoor Space
Public spaces shall be legally secured for public access and use.
Such spaces may include plazas, courtyards, atriums, or similar areas designed to accommodate public gathering or pedestrian circulation.
Spaces shall be accessible during hours determined by the City and maintained at the owner's expense.
Green Roofs
Green roofs shall exceed any minimum landscaping or sustainability requirements otherwise required by this bylaw.
Green roofs shall be designed to meet recognized industry standards for performance, maintenance, and longevity.
Preference shall be given to green roofs that are accessible or that demonstrably contribute to stormwater management, urban heat reduction, or biodiversity.
Additional Open Space
Open space provided under this subsection shall be in addition to any minimum landscaped open space otherwise required by this bylaw.
Open space shall be usable, well-designed, and appropriately landscaped.
Priority shall be given to open space that improves pedestrian connectivity or contributes to the public realm.
Natural Heritage Enhancement and Urban Canopy
The provision or restoration of natural heritage features that deliver a demonstrable public benefit, including the establishment or enhancement of tree cover equivalent to a minimum of fifteen percent (15%) of the lot area, using
The design review process shall apply to building Height Bonus, as described in Section 2.10 following a typical Site Plan approval submission requirements.
In considering a new application for bonus height, the Design Review Board may consider any requested changes from the Built Form requirements of the zone on a case by case basis for the additional floors only.
The developer shall provide for consideration their calculations for the assessed value of the additional gross floor area following Section 2.11.2 and the developer's assessed value of the proposed public benefit including their methodology for valuation.
Design Review Process
Upon receipt of the completed application and related application fee for a design review, the plans shall first be reviewed by the Development Officer to confirm compliance with all other applicable provisions of this bylaw.
Compliant applications shall be forwarded to a design reviewer, no less than one week before the design review meeting. Each design reviewer shall, prior to the meeting,:
Conduct a review of the proposed Development for conformance with the intent of this bylaw, including the Design Standards for the 500 Lot Area and the criteria for evaluation for design review.
For Designated Heritage Resources, subject to the regulations of the Heritage Preservation Bylaw, the design reviewer shall also review the Heritage Assessment Report prepared by the Heritage Officer.
The Development Officer or designate is authorized, with cause, to enter any land, building, or structure in the City, provided such entry is not excessive or by force, is at a reasonable time, and is for the purpose of making an inspection or examination relating to this bylaw.
Bylaw enforcement may be undertaken by the City in accordance with Planning Act.
The Development Officer may apply to a Justice of the Peace or provincial Court Judge for an order authorizing entry onto a property in the event that any person refuses or does not Permit an inspection to be carried out pursuant to this bylaw.
The provincial Court Judge or Justice of the Peace may at any time issue a warrant in the prescribed form authorizing a person named in the warrant to enter and search a building, receptacle or place if the Provincial Court Judge or Justice of the Peace is satisfied by information on oath that there are reasonable grounds to believe that an offence is being committed and the entry will afford evidence relevant to an offence, and such a search warrant shall name the date upon which it expires which shall be not later than fifteen (15) days after the warrant is issued.
In addition to any fine or penalty imposed, the Provincial Court of Prince Edward Island may order the person convicted to Restore the premises or land on which the offence has occurred to its original condition or to the satisfaction of the authority having jurisdiction, unless such restoration will constitute a safety or health hazard.
Any person who, being the owner or occupant of any land, building, or structure to which this bylaw applies, fails to:
Remove a sign;
Comply with City bylaw provisions;
Obtain a permit;
Cease work on or restore a building or property to its original condition, when Development has been undertaken; or
Obtain a proper subdivision approval; In contravention of this bylaw is guilty of an offence and liable under conviction to a fine and, in default of payment, to a term of imprisonment
Any person who impedes, attempts to impede, refuses or does not permit inspection of a property pursuant to this bylaw shall be guilty of an offence.
Where a person convicted under this section fails to commence the restoration ordered within sixty (60) days after the order has been made, the City may take such steps as it deems necessary to Restore or remove the subject matter of the offence at the expense of the owner.
When an offence under this bylaw is committed or continued for more than one (1) day, the person who committed the offence is liable to be convicted for a separate offence for each day on which the offence is committed or continued.
A person, contractor or owner who violates this bylaw is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding five thousand dollars ($5,000) in each case together with the cost of prosecution, and, in default of payment of the fine and costs, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three (3) months unless the fine and costs together with the costs of enforcing the same are sooner paid.
Where the offence is a continuing offence in addition to the penalties provided in this section, such person shall be liable for all costs in immediate removal of such an offence, and the Provincial Judge may impose a penalty not exceeding five thousand dollars ($5,000) for every day the said offence continues and in default of payment thereof to imprisonment not exceeding thirty (30) days.
Where there is default of payment, any person or corporate officer is liable to be imprisoned in accordance with the Summary Convictions Act.
A person who is dissatisfied that staff or Council are not following the procedures and requirements of this bylaw, may appeal to
No building or structure shall be demolished without obtaining a demolition permit.
A demolition permit may be issued for a building or structure, or a part thereof, unless the property on which the building or structure is located is identified as a Designated Heritage Resource or is located within a Heritage Preservation Area, as per the Heritage Preservation Bylaw.
Debris from a demolition site shall be hauled to and disposed of at a provincially approved location with signed assurance to that effect provided by the owner.
The foundation of a demolition site shall be levelled to grade, unless the demolition permit was approved in conjunction with an approved building permit for the same property.
An application for a demolition permit shall include the submission of a damage deposit, in an amount set from time to time in the City Fees document.
No building or structure shall be moved through, into, or out of the City without obtaining a moving permit.
An application for a moving permit shall include:
The transportation route within City boundaries;
The date and time of the proposed move; and
An approved building permit for a building moving onto a property within the City.
When a building is moved off of a property within the City, the foundation shall be levelled to grade, unless the moving permit was approved in conjunction with an approved building permit for the same property.
An application for a moving permit shall include the submission of a damage deposit, in an amount set from time to time by Council.

The Development Officer may apply to a Justice of the Peace or provincial Court Judge for an order authorizing entry onto a property in the event that any person refuses or does not Permit an inspection to be carried out pursuant to this bylaw.

No building or structure shall be demolished without obtaining a demolition permit.

A demolition permit may be issued for a building or structure, or a part thereof, unless the property on which the building or structure is located is identified as a Designated Heritage Resource or is located within a Heritage Preservation Area, as per the Heritage Preservation Bylaw.
Debris from a demolition site shall be hauled to and disposed of at a provincially approved location with signed assurance to that effect provided by the owner.

The date and time of the proposed move; and

An application for a moving permit shall include the submission of a damage deposit, in an amount set from time to time by Council.

map_references_raw.text_raw: Current zoning map raster package represented in PostGIS-compatible spatial artifacts.

review_flags.description: PDF text order was extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and table layout before normalization.
Some extracted text was not safely assigned to a labeled section and is preserved in content_blocks.
